Control Arm.evidence: In the 'METHODS' section on page 1, it states: "We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone." The 'ADT alone' group serves as the comparison group that does not receive the experimental treatment (ADT plus docetaxel).

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: On page 1, under the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone.' Treatment Arm 1 is ADT plus docetaxel.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Treatment Arm(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone.' Table 1 on page 5 also clearly lists 'ADT plus Docetaxel' and 'ADT Alone' as the two arms.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'A total of 790 patients (median age, 63 years) underwent randomization.' This is corroborated on page 4, in the 'RESULTS' section under 'PATIENTS', which states: 'From July 2006 through December 2012, a total of 790 patients were enrolled and underwent randomization.' Additionally, Table 1 on page 5 lists 'All patients' with 'No. of Patients' as '790'.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel ... or ADT alone.' This indicates two treatment arms. Table 1 on page 5 also clearly lists 'ADT plus Docetaxel' and 'ADT Alone' as the two groups.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states, 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel... or ADT alone.' This identifies the experimental arm as 'ADT plus docetaxel'. On page 5, Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients,' lists 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)', indicating 397 participants in this arm.

Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 5, Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under the column 'ADT Alone', the total number of patients (N) is listed as 393.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: On page 1, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'After a median follow-up of 28.9 months, the median overall survival was 13.6 months longer with ADT plus docetaxel (combination therapy) than with ADT alone'. On page 4, in the 'RESULTS' section, it states: 'the median follow-up was 28.9 months'. On page 6, in the caption for 'Figure 1. Kaplan-Meier Estimates of Overall Survival', it states: 'The median duration of follow-up was 28.9 months among all patients (Panel A)'.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles), with premedication with 8 mg of oral dexamethasone at 12 hours, 3 hours, and 1 hour before docetaxel infusion.
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel (at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area every 3 weeks for six cycles) or ADT alone.' This is further detailed on page 2 under 'TREATMENT PLAN, STRATIFICATION, AND RANDOMIZATION': 'Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg per square meter of body-surface area given every 3 weeks for six cycles, with premedication with 8 mg of oral dexamethasone at 12 hours, 3 hours, and 1 hour before docetaxel infusion.' This describes the specific regimen for Treatment Arm 1 (combination therapy).

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document describes various outcomes such as overall survival, time to progression, and adverse events, but there is no mention of 'Quality of Life' or 'QoL' assessments or results reported in the abstract, methods, or results sections.

Trial.evidence: The 'STUDY OVERSIGHT' section on page 2 states: 'The primary objective of the E3805 study was to determine whether docetaxel therapy at the beginning of ADT for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer would result in longer overall survival than that with ADT alone.' The 'CONCLUSIONS' section on page 1 also mentions 'Clinical Trials.gov number, NCT00309985'.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document focuses on overall survival, time to progression, and adverse events. While ECOG performance status is mentioned, it is a measure of functional status/disability, not a comprehensive 'Quality of Life Scale' reflecting overall well-being and satisfaction with health status as assessed through a standardized questionnaire or survey. No other mentions of a quality of life scale or similar assessment were found.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under 'Volume of metastases - no. (%)', row 'High', column 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under 'Volume of metastases - no. (%)', row 'High', column 'ADT Alone (N=393)'.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline Characteristics of the Patients', under 'Volume of metastases - no. (%)', row 'Low', column 'ADT Alone (N=393)'

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: On page 4, in the 'RESULTS' section under 'PATIENTS', it states: 'The median age was 64 years (range, 36 to 88) in the combination group'. The combination group is the treatment arm. This is confirmed in Table 1 on page 5, under 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)', 'Age — yr', 'Median' is 64.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: On page 4, in the 'RESULTS' section under 'PATIENTS', it states: 'and 63 years (range, 39 to 91) in the ADT-alone group'. The ADT-alone group is the control arm. This is confirmed in Table 1 on page 5, under 'ADT Alone (N=393)', 'Age — yr', 'Median' is 63.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The document discusses 'metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer' but does not distinguish or report results based on synchronous versus metachronous metastases.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The document discusses 'metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer' but does not distinguish or report results based on synchronous versus metachronous metastases.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: On page 2, under 'METHODS', it states that 'stratification was performed prospectively according to high or low burden of metastatic disease'. On page 3, under 'PATIENTS', it defines 'high volume [defined as the presence of visceral metastases or >=4 bone lesions with >=1 beyond the vertebral bodies and pelvis]'. Figure 1B and Figure 2 explicitly present results for 'Patients with High-Volume Disease' and 'Volume of metastases - High' subgroup, respectively.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: On page 2, under 'METHODS', it states that 'stratification was performed prospectively according to high or low burden of metastatic disease'. On page 3, under 'PATIENTS', it defines 'low volume' as the counterpart to high volume. Figure 1C and Figure 2 explicitly present results for 'Patients with Low-Volume Disease' and 'Volume of metastases - Low' subgroup, respectively.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The document reports 'median overall survival' (57.6 months for treatment arm) and 'median time to biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic progression' (20.2 months for combination group), but does not explicitly state the 'median on-treatment duration' for the entire therapy in the treatment arm. Docetaxel was given for a fixed 6 cycles, but the ADT component is ongoing.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The document reports 'median overall survival' (44.0 months for ADT alone group) and 'median time to biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic progression' (11.7 months for ADT-alone group), but does not explicitly state the 'median on-treatment duration' for the entire therapy in the control arm (ADT alone).

NCT.evidence: Found in the "CONCLUSIONS" section on page 1, explicitly stated as "ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT00309985".

PubMed ID.evidence: PubMed ID is not explicitly reported in the document. The DOI is provided, but not the PubMed ID.

Trial Name.value: E3805 study (Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer)
Trial Name.evidence: The article title is "Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer". The "STUDY OVERSIGHT" section on page 2 explicitly refers to "the E3805 study" as the trial.

Author.evidence: The author list is provided at the beginning of the article on page 1. Using "et al." for brevity as requested.

Year.evidence: The publication year is explicitly stated on page 1 as "This article was published on August 5, 2015" and "N Engl J Med 2015".

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document contains extensive sections (Background, Methods, Results, Conclusions, multiple pages, tables, figures) typical of a full publication, not just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: The study design, involving a large number of patients (790) and comparing two treatment arms for overall survival, is characteristic of a Phase 3 trial. Additionally, the text on page 9 refers to a "previously reported randomized study... which had a similar design to that of the current study" as a "phase 3 trial", implying the current study is also Phase 3. Reference 5 also mentions a "SWOG phase III trial" in relation to the S9346 trial, which is mentioned in the context of the E3805 study on page 4.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The article presents the primary results and analysis of the E3805 study, including an interim analysis and a cutoff date for data, indicating it is the original report of the trial's findings.

Primary Endpoint(s).value: The primary objective was to test the hypothesis that the median overall survival would be 33.3% longer among patients receiving docetaxel added to ADT early during therapy than among patients receiving ADT alone.
Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: The value is explicitly stated in the 'METHODS' section on page 1: 'The primary objective was to test the hypothesis that the median overall survival would be 33.3% longer among patients receiving docetaxel added to ADT early during therapy than among patients receiving ADT alone.'

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Treatment.evidence: Derived from Table 1, under "ECOG performance status - no. (%)‡", by summing the values for rows "1" (114 (28.7%)) and "2" (6 (1.5%)) in the "ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)" column on page 5. (114 + 6 = 120, and 28.7% + 1.5% = 30.2%).

PS - N (%) | 1-2 | Control.evidence: Derived from Table 1, under "ECOG performance status - no. (%)‡", by summing the values for rows "1" (115 (29.3%)) and "2" (6 (1.5%)) in the "ADT Alone (N=393)" column on page 5. (115 + 6 = 121, and 29.3% + 1.5% = 30.8%).

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, row 'Gleason score - no. (%)', sub-rows '4-6' and '7' under 'ADT plus Docetaxel (N=397)'. The count is 21 + 96 = 117, and the percentage is 5.3% + 24.2% = 29.5%.

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, row 'Gleason score - no. (%)', sub-rows '4-6' and '7' under 'ADT Alone (N=393)'. The count is 21 + 83 = 104, and the percentage is 5.3% + 21.1% = 26.4%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: From Table 1 on page 5, 397 patients were assigned to the 'ADT plus Docetaxel' arm. The title of Table 3 on page 8 states 'Adverse Events of Grade 3 or Higher among the 390 Patients Who Received the Docetaxel-Containing Regimen'. This indicates that 390 patients in the treatment arm received docetaxel. The percentage is calculated as (390/397)*100 = 98.24%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: From Table 1 on page 5, 393 patients were assigned to the 'ADT Alone' arm. In the 'ASSIGNED THERAPY ADMINISTERED AND SUBSEQUENT THERAPY' section on page 8, it is stated that '137 patients of these patients had received docetaxel for castration-resistant prostate cancer, and another 10 patients had received docetaxel before castration resistance was confirmed.' Therefore, a total of 137 + 10 = 147 patients in the control arm received docetaxel. The percentage is calculated as (147/393)*100 = 37.40%.

No. of Deaths - N | Treatment.evidence: On page 4, under the 'RESULTS' section, it states: '...with 136 deaths in the ADT-alone group and 101 deaths in the combination group.' The combination group refers to the ADT plus docetaxel (treatment) arm.

No. of Deaths - N | Control.evidence: On page 4, under the 'RESULTS' section, it states: '...with 136 deaths in the ADT-alone group and 101 deaths in the combination group.' The ADT-alone group refers to the control arm.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: The proportion of patients who had a decrease in the PSA level to less than 0.2 ng per milliliter at 12 months; the median time to the development of castration-resistant prostate cancer (biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic); and the median time to clinical progression.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: The 'SECONDARY END POINTS AND TOXIC EFFECTS' section on page 6 explicitly states: 'The proportion of patients who had a decrease in the PSA level to less than 0.2 ng per milliliter at 12 months was 27.7% in the combination group, as compared with 16.8% in the ADT-alone group (P<0.001) (Table 2). The median time to the development of castration-resistant prostate cancer (biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic) was 20.2 months with combination therapy, as compared with 11.7 months with ADT alone (hazard ratio in the combination group, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.51 to 0.72; P<0.001) (Table 2, and Fig. S2A in the Supplementary Appendix). The median time to clinical progression was 33.0 months with combination therapy, as compared with 19.8 months with ADT alone (hazard ratio, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.50 to 0.75; P<0.001) (Table 2, and Fig. S2B in the Supplementary Appendix).'

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: The document states that the study enrolled 'men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer' (Page 1, Methods), meaning all patients had metastases at the time of study entry. However, it does not provide a breakdown of how many of these patients had metastases specifically 'at the time of diagnosis of prostate cancer' (synchronous) versus those who developed them 'after the diagnosis of prostate cancer' (metachronous) as a baseline characteristic for the treatment arm. Therefore, the specific number and percentage for synchronous metastases in the treatment arm are not reported.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: 'We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel... or ADT alone.' This indicates docetaxel is the additional therapy given in combination with the primary treatment (ADT).

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document describes a single randomized controlled trial (RCT) on chemohormonal therapy. It details the methods, results, and conclusions of this specific study, including adverse events and efficacy. However, it does not provide an overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCTs) in a general or methodological sense. The document focuses on the findings of its own trial rather than a broader assessment of evidence criteria.

Treatment Class.evidence: The title of the article is "Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer". The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states that participants were assigned to receive "ADT plus docetaxel" or "ADT alone". 'ADT plus docetaxel' represents a chemohormonal therapy, while 'ADT alone' represents a hormonal therapy.
